records.title: Average revenue per paying user
records.summary: Track ARPPU by title, platform, and monetization model to optimize pricing and in-game economy balance.
records.metric_definition: Total in-app purchase and subscription revenue divided by unique paying users in the period.
records.methodology_note: Separate cosmetic, battle pass, and gacha revenue streams for granular view.

records.title: Player retention curves
records.summary: Measure D1, D7, and D30 retention by title and acquisition cohort to benchmark engagement and identify drop-off points.
records.metric_definition: Players returning on day N divided by players who installed on day 0.

records.title: Player safety and age-appropriate design
records.summary: Implement age verification, parental controls, and content moderation safeguards aligned with child safety standards.

records.title: Fair monetization transparency
records.summary: Disclose loot-box probabilities, spending caps, and in-game economy mechanics to maintain player trust and regulatory compliance.

records.title: Live-service engagement optimization
records.summary: Design data-driven content calendars, events, and season passes that sustain engagement and spending velocity across the player lifecycle.

records.title: Cross-platform player economics
records.summary: Unify player identity and spending data across mobile, console, and PC to measure true lifetime value and optimize cross-platform offers.

records.title: Build use cases on real-time player telemetry for live-service engagement
records.summary: Prioritize use cases that consume real-time player telemetry streams — session events, progression milestones, economy transactions — for live-service engagement tuning rather than next-day batch analysis that misses optimal intervention windows.

records.title: Produce cross-title player value metrics reusable across studios
records.summary: Prefer use cases that output unified player lifetime value, engagement scores, and spending profiles reusable across studios and franchises rather than title-specific metrics that prevent portfolio-level decisions.
